overview: Madness Burn is a two-color Rakdos Pauper Burn variant that leverages Looting effects alongside cards with the Madness mechanic to generate virtual card advantage and resilience. By combining direct damage spells with recurring threats like Sneaky Snacker, it maintains a permanent aggressive clock while offering more flexibility than monocolored Burn. The deck aims to dictate the format's speed and outlast opponents through graveyard recursion and efficient mana fixing.
win_conditions: Apply early pressure with Kitchen Imp and Sneaky Snacker to set a permanent aggressive clock
Deal direct damage with Lightning Bolt, Fiery Temper, and Chain Lightning to close out games
Recursively cast Sneaky Snacker from the graveyard using Looting effects to bypass removal and maintain board presence

keep_criteria: Hands with early damage spells or creatures to establish the aggressive clock
Hands containing Looting effects paired with Madness cards to enable virtual card advantage
Hands with at least two lands to support early sequencing
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (preferably fetchable or duals), at least one discard outlet (Alms of the Vein, Vampire's Kiss, or Grab the Prize), and 1-2 cheap burn spells.
Keep if you can play a land and cast an outlet or burn on turn 1 or 2.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early interaction or damage to set the clock
Hands with too many lands or late-game cards that delay the aggressive strategy
Mulligan on pure burn with no outlets, pure outlets with no mana, or hands with 3+ lands and no play.

examples.reason: Provides immediate clock, early looting synergy, and land drop for turn 1/2 sequencing

early_game: Sequence spells carefully to drop Razortrap Gorge untapped by turn 3 or 4
Play Kitchen Imp and Sneaky Snacker immediately to establish an aggressive clock
Cast Looting effects like Faithless Looting or Grab the Prize to trigger Madness and draw into threats
Turn 1: Play land, cast discard outlet if mana allows, or play land.
Turn 2: Cast second outlet or burn spell.
Sequence: Cast outlet -> opponent discards or you discard -> cast burn from graveyard via Madness -> repeat.
Prioritize finding more outlets over burn early to establish the engine.
mid_game: Use Alms of the Vein for life gain in Aggro mirrors while dealing damage
Deploy Voldaren Epicure to create a token that enables further Madness triggers
Utilize Highway Robbery or Vampire’s Kiss for additional damage and discard synergy
Hold mana for instant-speed burn responses, but commit to casting burn via Madness as soon as you have a discard outlet on the battlefield.
Never hoard burn spells in hand
intentionally discard them to fuel the graveyard.
Track your graveyard burn count and opponent's life total closely.
This is a face-race deck with minimal board presence.
late_game: Reuse Faithless Looting from the graveyard to cycle hand or enable more Sneaky Snacker copies
Play Stormshriek Feral as an extra threat and discard outlet
Leverage Rakdos Carnarium to reuse Jagged Barrens or Bojuka Bog ETB effects for mana fixing and damage
Lethal burn spells cast from the graveyard via Madness.
Chain multiple cheap burns (Shock, Lightning Strike, Lightning Helix, etc.) to close out the game.
Use discard outlets to dig for the final burn spell if needed.
Go all-in when you have 2+ burn spells in the graveyard and a discard outlet on the battlefield, or when you're at 3 or less life and can deal lethal this turn.
Commit fully if the opponent has no removal or counterspells in hand.

key_cards.name: Kitchen Imp
key_cards.notes: Has Haste and Flying, acting as efficient early damage and a recurring clock.

key_cards.name: Sneaky Snacker
key_cards.notes: Returns from graveyard via Madness, bypassing traditional interactions and maintaining pressure.

key_cards.name: Faithless Looting
key_cards.notes: Draws two cards for one mana, triggers Madness twice, and can be reused from graveyard to cycle or enable Snacker.

key_cards.name: Lightning Bolt
key_cards.notes: Flexible removal and damage spell to interact with the board or close games.

key_cards.name: Jagged Barrens
key_cards.notes: Functions as a land that deals extra damage on ETB, reusable via Rakdos Carnarium.

key_cards.name: Razortrap Gorge
key_cards.notes: Helps fix mana in the two-color deck and can enter untapped by turn 3 or 4 with correct sequencing.

key_cards.name: Bojuka Bog
key_cards.notes: Efficient maindeck graveyard disruption that enters tapped but supports the slower, resilient Burn plan.

key_cards.name: Rakdos Carnarium
key_cards.notes: Allows reuse of Jagged Barrens or Bojuka Bog ETB effects while fixing mana.

key_cards.name: Stormshriek Feral
key_cards.notes: New discard outlet that functions as an immediate-impact creature in longer games.

key_cards.name: Voldaren Epicure
key_cards.notes: Deals damage, puts a body on board, and creates a token to discard/draw, enabling Madness effects.

sequencing_tips: Sequence early spells carefully to ensure Razortrap Gorge enters untapped by turn 3 or 4
Prioritize casting Looting effects before or alongside Madness cards to maximize virtual card advantage
Save Faithless Looting for the graveyard to reuse it in longer games for cycling or enabling Sneaky Snacker
Use Alms of the Vein strategically in Aggro mirrors to gain life while maintaining damage output
Common mistake: Hoarding burn spells in hand instead of discarding them to fuel the graveyard.
Remember, discard outlets are your engine
If you discard a burn spell to an opponent's effect, it still goes to the graveyard and can be cast via Madness.
Ensure you have priority to cast from the graveyard, and watch out for 'discard to graveyard' vs 'discard to graveyard for Madness' timing.
In Unknown format, verify format-specific restrictions, but the core loop remains consistent.

matchups.advice: Sideboard in Pyroblast and Nihil Spellbomb to counter blue spells and graveyard reliance
Sideboard in Pyroblast to handle the aggressive blue creature strategy
Sideboard in Gorilla Shaman to delay artifact turns and disrupt the combo
Sideboard in Duress and Extract a Confession to remove key non-creature spells and threats
Adjust sideboard to handle traditional burn threats and maintain clock pressure

Use discard to strip counterspells from hand before committing. Cast burn spells in response to their counterspells if you have priority, or sequence your discard outlets to force them to counter multiple spells. Rely on graveyard recursion to bypass single-target counters.

Apply maximum early pressure. Do not let them stabilize. Use discard to hit their mana dorks, land drops, or early threats. If they get a big board, burn it down or race past it. Prioritize life total over board presence.

You are the aggro. Do not slow down. Use discard to disrupt their early plays and key threats. If they get too wide, use any available burn to clear key threats, but prioritize closing the game over board control.

Pressure relentlessly. Discard their removal and counterspells. If they try to grind, you must win before they find their answer. Use discard to strip key answers, then burn through their life total. Avoid trading burn for their removal unless it's lethal.

Race to discard. Prioritize keeping discard outlets. Use your own discard to strip their outlets and burn spells. Win by having more burn in the graveyard, better sequencing, or out-drawing them with your outlets. Respect their discard and time your own carefully.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells, board wipes, and opponent discard outlets.
Note if the opponent is playing draw spells (they're trying to out-value you) or discard (they're disrupting your graveyard).

overview_evidence.evidence_span: takes advantage of the synergy of Looting effects with cards like Madness and Sneaky Snacker... serving as one of the main means of dictating the format's speed while, by having access to Looting effects and a second color, it can have more flexibility and resilience

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Kitchen Imp and Sneaky Snacker are cheap creatures with high power that set the permanent aggressive clock
Fiery Temper and Alms of the Vein function as extra Lightning Bolts and Lava Spikes in the list
Snacker is the main payoff for playing Madness today... guarantees resilience in keeping the clock active every turn

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Razortrap Gorge has a good chance of entering this list untapped by turn 3 or 4 if we sequence our spells correctly in the early game
Faithless Looting... can be reused from the graveyard to remove useless cards from the hand in longer games, or to enable more copies of Sneaky Snacker

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Pyroblast remains the best tool against Blue Terror and Faeries... Duress is the standard answer against Bogles, Affinity... Contaminated Ground plays mainly against Tron... Nihil Spellbomb fits into any game that relies too much on the graveyard, like Mono Blue Terror and Dredge.
